claims.claim: High entropy plus high fill can stutter if semantic persistence and high-fill pruning floors conflict.
claims.rationale: Existing Minime semantic-bus tests and source verify high-entropy stale-window extension, high-fill persistence, salience/hysteresis, and current-fill retention behavior without requiring a runtime retune.

claims.claim: A low-fill 20 percent / entropy 0.90 case should preserve semantic persistence long enough for deep thought rather than pruning as ordinary stale text.
claims.rationale: The semantic stale-window formula already extends low-fill, high-entropy windows; existing tests cover the entropy multiplier and current-fill survival edges.

claims.claim: Adding `density_gradient_weight` to semantic entropy persistence could favor depth retention over breadth when density-gradient is shallow and entropy is high.
claims.rationale: This would alter live Minime sensory cadence and semantic memory retention, so it needs Mike/operator approval plus post-restart health/fill/sensory monitoring before implementation.
